#
# /etc/pacman.conf
#
# See the pacman.conf(5) manpage for option and repository directives

#
# GENERAL OPTIONS
#
[options]
# The following paths are commented out with their default values listed.
# If you wish to use different paths, uncomment and update the paths.
#RootDir     = /
#DBPath      = /var/lib/pacman/
#CacheDir    = /var/cache/pacman/pkg/
#LogFile     = /var/log/pacman.log
#GPGDir      = /etc/pacman.d/gnupg/
#HookDir     = /etc/pacman.d/hooks/
HoldPkg     = pacman glibc
#XferCommand = /usr/bin/curl -L -C - -f -o %o %u
#XferCommand = /usr/bin/wget --passive-ftp -c -O %o %u
#CleanMethod = KeepInstalled
Architecture = auto

# Pacman won't upgrade packages listed in IgnorePkg and members of IgnoreGroup
#IgnorePkg   =
#IgnoreGroup =

#NoUpgrade   =
#NoExtract   =

# Misc options
#UseSyslog
#Color
#NoProgressBar
CheckSpace
#VerbosePkgLists
ParallelDownloads = 5
DownloadUser = alpm
#DisableSandboxFilesystem
#DisableSandboxSyscalls

# By default, pacman accepts packages signed by keys that its local keyring
# trusts (see pacman-key and its man page), as well as unsigned packages.
SigLevel    = Required DatabaseOptional
LocalFileSigLevel = Optional
#RemoteFileSigLevel = Required

# NOTE: You must run `pacman-key --init` before first using pacman; the local
# keyring can then be populated with the keys of all official Arch Linux
# packagers with `pacman-key --populate archlinux`.

#
# REPOSITORIES
#   - can be defined here or included from another file
#   - pacman will search repositories in the order defined here
#   - local/custom mirrors can be added here or in separate files
#   - repositories listed first will take precedence when packages
#     have identical names, regardless of version number
#   - URLs will have $repo replaced by the name of the current repo
#   - URLs will have $arch replaced by the name of the architecture
#
# Repository entries are of the format:
#       [repo-name]
#       Server = ServerName
#       Include = IncludePath
#
# The header [repo-name] is crucial - it must be present and
# uncommented to enable the repo.
#

# The testing repositories are disabled by default. To enable, uncomment the
# repo name header and Include lines. You can add preferred servers immediately
# after the header, and they will be used before the default mirrors.

#[core-testing]
#Include = /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist

[core]
Include = /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist

#[extra-testing]
#Include = /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist

[extra]
Include = /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist

# If you want to run 32 bit applications on your x86_64 system,
# enable the multilib repositories as required here.

#[multilib-testing]
#Include = /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist

[multilib]
Include = /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist

# An example of a custom package repository.  See the pacman manpage for
# tips on creating your own repositories.
#[custom]
#SigLevel = Optional TrustAll
#Server = file:///home/custompkgs

This is a classic error that almost always happens right after enabling a new repository like multilib.

What happened is that pacman tried to download the database and its security signature, but instead of the actual file, it received a corrupted file, an empty file, or an HTML 404 error page from your current mirror. Because it's not a valid PGP signature, GPGME throws the "No data" error and halts.

Here is how to clear the corrupted files and force a clean sync.
Step 1: Delete the corrupted sync databases

You need to manually delete the cached repository databases so pacman is forced to start from scratch.

Run this command:
Bash

sudo rm -rf /var/lib/pacman/sync/*

Step 2: Force a complete refresh

Normally, -Syu only downloads databases if it thinks they are outdated. By adding a second y, you force pacman to bypass its checks and redownload everything fresh.

Run this:
Bash

sudo pacman -Syyu

Step 3: (If it still fails) Update your Mirrorlist

If you run the command above and still get the exact same error, it means the top mirror in your /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist

❯ sudo pacman -Syu
error: GPGME error: No data
:: Synchronizing package databases...
 core is up to date
 extra is up to date
 multilib                                                                                                                                    2.2 KiB  5.72 KiB/s 00:00 [#######################################################################################################] 100%
error: GPGME error: No data
error: failed to synchronize all databases (invalid or corrupted database (PGP signature))
❯ sudoedit /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist
❯ yay -Syu
error: GPGME error: No data
:: Synchronizing package databases...
 core is up to date
 extra is up to date
 multilib                                                                                                                                    2.2 KiB  7.79 KiB/s 00:00 [#######################################################################################################] 100%
error: GPGME error: No data
error: failed to synchronize all databases (invalid or corrupted database (PGP signature))
 -> error refreshing databases - exit status 1

unction Status:name()
    local h = self._tab.current.hovered
    if not h then
        return ui.Line({})
    end
    return ui.Line(" " .. tostring(h.url))
end

This is a very common headache! The reason this happens is that Wine is doing two tricky things at once:

    It is likely running through XWayland (the X11 compatibility layer) rather than native Wayland.

    It operates inside a simulated Windows "prefix" that has its own fake Windows registry, which often completely ignores your Linux user settings.

Here is how to force Wine applications to respect your custom Hyprland cursor, ordered from the most likely fix to the most thorough.
1. Move the Cursor to the System Folder (The Most Likely Fix)

Wine (and tools like Lutris or Bottles) frequently fail to read cursor themes that are stored in your personal user folder (~/.local/share/icons/). They only reliably see cursors installed system-wide.

If you manually downloaded your cursor, you need to copy it to the root directory:
Bash

sudo cp -r ~/.local/share/icons/Your-Cursor-Theme-Name
sudo pacman -S qt5ct qt6ct kvantum kvantum breeze-icons

exec-once = gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface cursor-theme 'Your-Cursor-Theme-Name'
exec-once = gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface cursor-size 24

❯ systemctl --user --failed
  UNIT              LOAD   ACTIVE SUB    DESCRIPTION                              >
● mpd-mpris.service loaded failed failed mpd-mpris: An implementation of the MPRIS>

Legend: LOAD   → Reflects whether the unit definition was properly loaded.
        ACTIVE → The high-level unit activation state, i.e. generalization of SUB.
        SUB    → The low-level unit activation state, values depend on unit type.

#!/bin/bash
# Listen to the audio server for removed devices
pactl subscribe | grep --line-buffered "Event 'remove' on sink" | while read -r line; do
    # Pause all active media players
    playerctl -a pause
done

[Unit]
Description=Auto-pause media when Bluetooth disconnects
After=pipewire.service pulseaudio.service

[Service]
ExecStart=%h/.local/bin/bluetooth-auto-pause.sh
Restart=always
RestartSec=3

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

#for libadwaita gtk4 apps you can use this command:
exec = gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface color-scheme "prefer-dark"   # for GTK4 apps

#for gtk3 apps you need to install adw-gtk3 theme (in arch linux sudo pacman -S adw-gtk-theme)
exec = gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.interface gtk-theme "adw-gtk3"   # for GTK3 apps

#for kde apps you need to install: sudo pacman -S qt5ct qt6ct kvantum kvantum breeze-icons   
#you will need to set dark theme for qt apps from kde more difficult thans with gnome :D:
env = QT_QPA_PLATFORMTHEME,qt6ct   # for Qt apps# Theme

[themes.palette]
background = "#24273a"
foreground = "#cad3f5"
black = "#494d64"
red = "#ed8796"
green = "#a6da95"

bright_white = "#b8c0e0"

font_family JetBrainsMono Nerd Font

linux_display_server wayland

update_check_interval 0

font_size 11

enable_audio_bell no

shell 		  zsh
shell_integration disabled

cursor_shape block
cursor_blink_interval 0

url_style curly
disable_ligatures always
remember_window_size no


window_padding_width 	20

scrollbar_handle_opacity 	0
scrollbar_track_opacity 	0
scrollbar_track_hover_opacity 	0

confirm_os_window_close 0
paste_actions quote-urls-at-prompt,confirm

sync_to_monitor yes

copy_on_select clipboard

copy_on_select yes

# Special Colors
foreground            #cdd6f4
background            #1e1e2e
cursor                #f5e0dc
selection_foreground  #0f1117
selection_background  #9399b2

# Standard Colors
color0  #1e1e2e

# Red (Deeper, more "Rose Pine")

# Green (Emerald/Cyan lean)

# Window Blur/Transparency (Optional but recommended for Hyprland)
background_opacity 0.7
#map
clear_all_shortcuts yes
map ctrl+i send_text all \x1b[105;5u
map ctrl+shift+c copy_to_clipboard
map ctrl+shift+v paste_from_clipboard
map ctrl+equal  change_font_size all +2.0
map ctrl+minus  change_font_size all -2.0
map ctrl+shift+e kitten hints --type url
map ctrl+shift+h show_scrollback
scrollback_pager nvim -c 'set ft=man' -
map ctrl+shift+l>f kitten hints --type path --program @
map ctrl+shift+l>u kitten hints --type url
map ctrl+shift+l>l kitten hints --type line --program @
map ctrl+shift+l>w kitten hints --type word --program @
audio_visualization to true in your config to enable this feature. See config docs.
